prompt_md: An AI assistant is driving a multi-day legacy migration. It must reference a large, frequently-edited source tree and also honor a few non-negotiable rules (e.g. 'preserve the public /v1/orders response shape') stated at the very start. Earlier long sessions violated those rules after older turns were compressed into summaries-of-summaries. **What is the best way to keep the rules reliably in context, and why?**

decision_choices: Pin the non-negotiable rules as protected facts carried verbatim every call, and use retrieval for the source tree
Switch to the largest available context window and re-send the entire transcript and all files each call
Use a pure sliding window of the last 15 turns and nothing else
Summarize everything, including the rules, into a single running summary
reason_choices: Because pinned facts bypass lossy summarization while retrieval keeps the large, changing source tree out of the window until a file is needed
Because a larger window guarantees the model attends equally to every file regardless of position
Because only the most recent turns ever matter for a migration's correctness
Because compressing the rules into the summary makes them more durable over time
